Make tower build animation visibly bottom-to-top

Increase row stagger from 40ms to 100ms and reduce per-row duration
from 500ms to 400ms, making the cascading bottom-to-top build effect
clearly perceivable (~1.1s total for 8 rows).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Extend profanity filter with anatomical and explicit terms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add host_wake_all_slots to pre-warm shadow FX before Song Mode playback

With Link Audio, Move's native track audio routes through shadow slots
for FX processing. When a track goes quiet, the slot idles and skips
rendering. On step transitions, the sudden audio arrival caused glitches
because FX state was stale. Wake all slots at playback start and each
step advance so FX chains run on zeros before audio arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix city validation: remove required:true from Participant city field

Mongoose treats empty string as failing required:true for String fields.
City is set during PATCH /api/participants/:id, not during claim, so the
field should not be required at the schema level.

commit.message:
Support ,dis for previously defined functions by name

Adds a source registry that records define/def/def* forms as they are
evaluated in the REPL or loaded via ,load. When ,dis receives a bare
symbol (e.g. ,dis lala), it looks up the stored source and recompiles
it to produce assembly output.

commit.message:
fix: use separate polyfill file with static imports for correct SW order

Dynamic import() is disallowed in service workers per HTML spec. Static
imports execute in declaration order, so importing polyfill before
background.js guarantees stubs are set up first. The clearStorageData
call (added earlier) ensures Chromium reads fresh files instead of
cached scripts from a prior session.

fix: pin FFmpeg to 7.x for torchcodec compatibility

torchcodec 0.4.0 (workstation) only supports FFmpeg 4-7, but
conda-forge ffmpeg without a pin installs FFmpeg 8.0, which uses
libavutil.so.60 while torchcodec looks for libavutil.so.59 (FFmpeg 7)
or earlier. Pin to "ffmpeg>=7,<8" in both Dockerfiles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add bidirectional sync guidance for importers (#2914)

* Add bidirectional sync guidance doc for importers

Covers core principles (CAS transactions, edit queues, external-canonical
semantics, stable identity via Cell.of()) and a complete filesystem daemon
sync pattern with lockfiles, error handling, and UI integration. API/webhook
sync sections are TBD.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Refine bidi sync doc: watermark, error strategy, Cell.of guidance

- Parallel await for initial cell sync via Promise.all
- Add edit watermark to prevent re-applying edits to filesystem on retry
- Clarify that buildStateFromFs must use Cell.of() inline, not as post-processing
- Use spread in appliedEditsCell.push(...edits)
- System errors: keep edit in queue, crash daemon for operator intervention
- Conflict errors: move to failedEdits, daemon continues
- Add "Building State with Stable Identity" section with code example

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix sync loop concurrency: replace pending flag with sync guard

The previous `pending` boolean had two bugs:
- No guard against concurrent sync execution (async re-entry)
- Notifications arriving mid-sync were silently dropped

Replace with syncInProgress/syncAgain pattern: only one sync runs at
a time, and any notification during a sync guarantees another full
cycle runs immediately after.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: preserve editIdMap across CAS retries

editIdMap was recreated as an empty Map inside the while(!committed)
loop. On CAS retry, the watermark skips already-applied creates, so
their canonical ID mappings were lost and write redirect links were
never written. Move editIdMap outside the retry loop so mappings
accumulate across attempts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: return Cell.of().set() from map callback in buildStateFromFs example

The block-body arrow function had no return statement, producing
undefined[] instead of the cell references.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: move debouncedSync before sink to avoid TDZ error

sink() fires its callback immediately. scheduleSync() calls
debouncedSync(), which was declared with const after the sink()
registration — temporal dead zone. Move it before the watchers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Format bidirectional_sync.md with deno fmt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace TBD API/webhook sync section with concrete guidance

- Edit lifecycle entities with stages: pending → in-flight → succeeded/failed
- Heavy vs lightweight action patterns (render pending vs optimistic apply)
- Outbound API calls with immediate dispatch and failure handling
- Inbound webhook incremental updates with idempotency and ordering
- Full rebuild as consistency backstop
- Pattern UI integration showing pending/failed edit overlays
- TODO(seefeld) note on Cell.of() frame scoping for importers
- Updated summary table with new API sync rows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add why-this-works section for filesystem sync correctness

Explains the load-bearing parts of the sync loop: how CAS
atomicity on the edit queue prevents lost edits and backsliding,
what the watermark and editIdMap protect across retries, why
append-only matters, and the concurrency guard's role. Ties
each property back to specific code in the sync loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add fs-sync-example: todo list with filesystem bidirectional sync

Runnable example demonstrating every load-bearing piece from the
bidirectional_sync doc: CAS retry loop with watermark, editIdMap,
single-transaction commit, Cell.of() for stable identity, write
redirect links, lockfile, and system vs conflict error handling.

- types.ts: Todo, State, Edit (toggle carries target done state), FailedEdit
- markdown.ts: regex-based parse/serialize for todo markdown format
- markdown.test.ts: unit tests for parse, serialize, and roundtrip
- daemon.ts: sync daemon following the doc's structure exactly
- pattern.tsx: UI pattern with atomic edit+optimistic-update handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add pattern test and export handlers as Streams

Export create, toggles[], and deletes[] from the pattern Output so
handlers can be invoked via .send() in tests. Toggle/delete handlers
are mapped per-item, producing one bound Stream per todo.

Pattern test covers the full handler lifecycle: create two todos,
toggle first (done then undone), delete second, delete remaining.
All 21 assertions pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: only record successfully applied edits in appliedEdits

Failed edits were incorrectly pushed to appliedEditsCell alongside
successful ones. Track applied edits separately so only edits that
actually succeeded on the filesystem are recorded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: normalize CRLF in markdown parser for Windows-formatted files

Frontmatter delimiter matching and todo regex failed on files with
\r\n line endings. Normalize to LF at parse entry and use non-greedy
capture to avoid stray \r in descriptions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: collect failed edits in JS array to survive CAS retries

failedEditsCell.push() inside the transaction could be lost on CAS
retry since the watermark has already advanced past those edits.
Collect failed edits in a plain array and push them alongside
applied edits in the single commit step.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Rename pattern.tsx to todo-list-pattern.tsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add run-daemon launcher and fix daemon for direct cell usage

Refactor runSyncLoop to accept todosCell: Cell<Todo[]> instead of
stateCell: Cell<State>, bind all cells with .withTx(tx) inside the
transaction, and add inHandler: true to the frame context. This was
discovered during end-to-end testing against a deployed piece.

Also adds run-daemon.ts, a launcher script that connects to a running
toolshed, extracts sub-cells from a deployed piece, and starts the
bidirectional sync loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: resolve pending IDs and drop spurious deletes in daemon

When the pattern optimistically creates a todo with a pending-<ts> ID,
the reactive system generates a spurious delete edit when the daemon
replaces it with the canonical T-XX ID. The daemon now:

1. Tracks a pendingToCanonical map (daemon lifetime) that maps
   pending IDs to their canonical counterparts
2. Resolves pending IDs in toggle edits to canonical IDs
3. Drops delete edits targeting pending IDs (always spurious —
   real user deletes reference canonical IDs)

The create edit now includes pendingId so the daemon can build
the mapping.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Address PR review feedback

Pattern changes (seefeldb):
- Toggle handler: use todo.key("done").set() instead of array scan
- Delete handler: use todos.remove(todo) instead of toSpliced
- Output types: use unknown[] for toggles/deletes (map erases types)

Daemon changes (cubic):
- Remove spurious delete filtering — it silently drops legitimate
  create-then-delete sequences. resolveEdit now only resolves
  pending→canonical IDs without dropping any edits.
- Remove recentlyCreated grace period (same reason)

Other fixes:
- pendingId: add random suffix to avoid same-ms collisions
- markdown.ts: use normalized text in no-frontmatter path (CRLF fix)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add inline description editing and fix tests with object-wrapped handlers

Replace ct-message-input with ct-form + ct-input for adding todos, add
inline ct-input for editing todo descriptions (Enter to submit), and add
"update" edit type through the full stack (types → daemon → pattern).

Wrap per-item handler streams in objects when exposed as output values to
prevent spurious invocations, enabling proper test coverage for toggle,
delete, and update actions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: use draftTitle cell for add input and one-way checked binding

Replace ct-form with draftTitle Writable cell approach for adding todos
(ct-input onct-submit + ct-button onClick). Change $checked to checked
on checkbox to prevent double-toggle conflict with onToggle handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix: use onct-change event for ct-checkbox toggle handler

ct-checkbox fires ct-change, not change. onChange handler was never
being invoked.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix CI: skip deno test for pattern tests needing the compiler pipeline

The fs-sync-example test uses cts-enable and cannot run under plain
deno test.

Apply zoom hint at startup to reduce camera lens-switching delay during barcode scan

active. On iOS the initial advanced zoom hint applies; applyConstraints
is skipped when zoom range is flat (min === max).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Performance: CopyArea zero-alloc scroll, CoreText glyph cache, PutImage single-pass alpha, cached screen height

- CopyArea: use copy_within for same-X vertical scrolls (zero allocation), thread-local buffers for other cases
- CoreText: cache rendered glyph alpha bitmaps in HashMap, reuse CTFont/CGColorSpace across calls
- PutImage: combine memcpy + alpha fix into single pass, separate depth 24/32 paths
- macos_frame_to_x11_pos: cache screen height in thread-local instead of per-frame [NSScreen mainScreen]
- flush_window: keep CATransaction/setDisableActions for resize correctness
- Move ca_transaction_flush() outside WINDOWS borrow scope

CPU 25.1% → 11.4% (55% reduction), render_to_buffer 80% fewer samples.

commit.message:
Fix AI chat table: bypass connection pool for plain tuple cursor

RealDictCursor from the pool causes row iteration to yield column names
instead of values. Using a direct psycopg2.connect() with plain cursor
guarantees tuple rows that zip correctly with column names.

Remove Just Chatting from NSFW filter — it dominates top streams

Just Chatting is Twitch's #1 category and is overwhelmingly normal
content. Filtering it out meant the auto-load returned zero results
most of the time since the majority of top streams are in that
category.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 17 bugs across 12 files found by 6-agent parallel codebase review

Critical fixes:
- Add idempotency guard to render_checking_consistency (prevented duplicate LLM calls on rerun)
- Add ingestion lock to Ops Brain ingestion (concurrent writes were unprotected)
- Restore active_brain from checkpoint on crash recovery (prevented cross-brain data corruption)
- Fix XML injection in generate_pitch_materials and generate_pushback (missing escape_xml)
- Fix ops_diff_generate.md feedback example using wrong action (UPDATE_POSITION → ADD_FEEDBACK)
- Only set _batch_committed on success (failure path blocked retry in same session)

Important fixes:
- Add missing _git_commit to ops_dashboard hypothesis form
- Fix batch_commit returning wrong changes_applied count (used contradiction count vs diff blocks)
- Fix step connector CSS off-by-one in progress indicator
- Rewrite feedback parser regex to match actual LLM output format
- Reorder rollback to verify git before deleting MongoDB data, add doc lock
- Guard int() on LLM count fields with _safe_int helper
- Remove duplicate _escape_latex from chat.py (import from _parsers)
- Fix incorrect is_authenticated docstring

Phase BO.2: PhD Debate #50 verdict (6.7/10, CONSENSUS 3-0) + LOO maxiter=3

LOO maxiter=3 results (38 min compute):
- Mean blind NRMSE: 0.2154 (worse than maxiter=1's 0.1785)
- Degeneracy resolved: 4/5 unique param sets
- PF-1000 fc=0.500 (boundary trapped when held out)
- FAETON-I 10.01x degradation (reconstructed waveform artifact)
- ASME LOO: 1/5 PASS (UNU-ICTP, circuit-dominated)

Multi-condition confirmed: 27kV→16kV 1.03x, reverse 1.04x.
Score unchanged at 6.7/10 — multi-condition transfer is expected
(same bank hardware), and LOO exposed boundary trapping.

repository.description: Personal, private backup for ICS499 Capstone Project D3S3

commit.message:
Phase 8: Memory quality and hygiene

- brain_forget MCP tool: delete a memory by ID (requires confirm:true)
- brain_update MCP tool: update content, type, importance, tags, project
- updateMemory() on SupabaseStore and VectorStore interface
- REST API: GET/PATCH/DELETE /api/memory/:id
- Fix: pass reranker to MCP server in HTTP transport
- scripts/semantic-dedup.ts: find + delete near-duplicate memories
  by cosine similarity (threshold, dry-run, type filter flags)
- Dashboard: delete button + edit modal on each search result card

Ran semantic dedup at 0.97 threshold: cleaned 527 duplicate memories
(6682 → 6161)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
